explanation: The phrase 'Exceeding sensor limits' translates to the atomic proposition 'limits'. The condition 'the pilot is not in control (not standby)' translates to '!standby'. The condition 'the system is supported without failures (not apfail)' translates to 'supported & !apfail'. These two conditions are combined with 'and', forming '(!standby & (supported & !apfail))'. The 'when' clause specifies a condition under which 'Exceeding sensor limits' triggers the action. Thus, the antecedent of the implication is 'limits & (!standby & (supported & !apfail))'. The phrase 'shall latch an autopilot pullup' implies that the 'pullup' state will eventually be entered and then remain true indefinitely (or until an unstated reset). This is represented by 'F G pullup' in LTL. The entire statement is a global requirement, indicating that this condition must hold at all times, leading to the 'G' (globally) operator wrapping the entire implication.
